message:
fix(core,fuse): fix ARC cache dirty tracking, add scope propagation, and expose link/symlink/setattr APIs

Dirty tracking bugfixes (bd-9vft):
- flush_pending_evictions now calls clear_dirty after writing blocks to device,
  preventing dirty count inflation and potential infinite flush loops
- All 3 flush paths no longer re-mark already-persisted blocks as dirty when
  repair notification fails — log-and-continue instead since repair is non-critical

Scope propagation fixes:
- FsOps::getattr, lookup, readdir, read, and write now properly thread
  RequestScope through to underlying read_inode/read_inode_attr calls instead
  of constructing empty scopes, enabling correct MVCC tracking
- read_dir() simplified to remove redundant RequestScope parameter
- fallocate mode parameter changed from u32 to i32 to match FUSE API contract

New public convenience methods on OpenFs:
- link(), symlink(), setattr() — delegate through FsOps with empty scope,
  matching the existing pattern for read/write/readlink/statfs

Test updates:
- resolve_extent and read_file_data call sites updated to pass &mut RequestScope
- Beads issue tracker updated with bd-9vft closure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
